scope_contract: Cleanliness-of-THIS-diff only. File-level findings tagged with introduced_in_diff. Pre-existing/historical dirt is ignored and non-blocking per Section 5.4 rule 3 / AC-2.6(b). Findings without introduced_in_diff:true are advisory/non-blocking by default-safe rule.

Pre-existing aggregate manifest in correct location; only modified.
scripts/ is correct for a guard script; kebab-case name matches convention (graphify-query.py, spec-check.py).
scripts/ correct for an operational shell script; kebab-case name fine.

summary.verdict: CLEAN — no file-organization cleanliness violations introduced by this cycle's diff. All 11 changed files are in correct locations with conformant naming. The new reference doc (venv-repair.md) was correctly placed in docs/reference/ rather than repo root. No temp files, build artifacts, duplicate scripts, or stray files detected in the diff scope.

limitations: Bash tool stdout rendering was degraded for part of this session; md5 byte-level dedup and mtime stat could not be machine-confirmed. This does not affect any verdict: duplicate-content would require a second identical file (none appears in git status or Glob), and mtime only matters for archive-candidate aging (none of these active just-created cycle artifacts are archive candidates). File existence and placement are confirmed authoritatively by git status (session-start snapshot) and Glob.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Skill(codex) returned no parseable response in this session (tool-output rendering degraded; Bash probes for codex availability also rendered empty). Treated as failed_parse per graceful-fallback contract.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted: ran 9-point adversarial self-review covering false positives/negatives, severity calibration, Safety-Rule compliance, --changed-files scope accuracy, docs/reference vs docs/guides bucketing, naming conventions, duplicate detection, temp/build-artifact detection, and the md5/mtime verification caveat. Self-review confirmed the zero-violation verdict; the verification caveat is recorded as a documented limitation rather than an 'unknown' finding because no condition under which it would change a verdict is present in the diff.
